questions.questionText:
A bird is flying close to the sea surface at a speed of 40 m/s. It decided to ascend and fly in a vertical circle. The speed of the bird varies with height h as

$v^2 = 1600 - 2gh$.

Its speed at the topmost point of the circle is 20 m/s. The acceleration of the bird in $\text{m/s}^2$ when its velocity is vertically up is [Take $g = 10~\text{m/s}^2$]
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: During the circular motion with constant speed :
options: Both velocity and acceleration are constant.
Velocity is constant but the acceleration changes.
Acceleration is constant but the velocity changes.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two racing cars of masses $m_1$ and $m_2$ are moving in circles of radii $r$ and $2r$ respectively and their angular speeds are equal. The ratio of the time taken by cars to complete one revolution is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle P is moving in a circle of radius 'a' with a uniform speed v. C is the centre of the circle and AB is a diameter. When passing through B the angular velocity of P about A and C are in the ratio
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle is performing circular motion of radius 1 m. Its speed is $v = (2t^2)$ ms$^{-1}$. What will be the magnitude of its acceleration at time $t = 1$ s?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle moves in a circle of radius 1 m . The particle moves with a constant angular speed of $1 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}$ about a fixed point lying on the circle's periphery. The velocity $v$ and the acceleration $a$ of the particle are
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The velocity and acceleration vectors of a particle undergoing circular motion are $\vec{v} = 2\hat{i} \ \text{m/s}$ and $\vec{a} = 2\hat{i} + 4\hat{j} \ \text{m/s}^2$ respectively at an instant of time. The radius of the circle is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A disc of radius 1 m rotates about a fixed axis passing through its centre (axis is perpendicular to the plane of the disc). Its rotation angle varies as $\theta = \sqrt{2} t^2$, where $t$ is in second and $\theta$ is in radian. The magnitude of acceleration of a point on its periphery at time $t = 1$ s is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In a watch, the seconds hand is 1 cm long. The magnitude of the change in velocity of its tip in 15 seconds is:
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two particles begin to move on the same circular path of radius 2 m from the same point P at t = 0. They have constant tangential accelerations $2 \text{ m/s}^2$ (clockwise) and $6 \text{ m/s}^2$ (anticlockwise), respectively. Let the point of their first meeting be Q. The smaller angle made by the chord PQ at the centre of the circle is:
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle is moving along a circular path of radius 40 cm. At an instant of time, when the acceleration vector makes an angle of $60^{\circ}$ with the radius vector, the speed of the particle is $2 \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{~s}^{-1}$ and the magnitude of acceleration $\mathrm{a}=10 \mathrm{n} \,\,\mathrm{m} \mathrm{s}^{-2}$. What is the value of n?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle is moving on circular path and it's angular velocity $\vec{\omega}=3 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}$ and position of particle with respect to center is $\vec{r}=\mathrm{x} \hat{i}-6 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}$. Find the value of x.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A wheel is of diameter 1 m . If it makes 30 revolutions $/ \mathrm{sec}$., the linear speed of a point on its circumference will be equal to $N \pi$ (in $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$ ). Find the value of $N$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: An aeroplane revolves in a circle above the surface of the earth at a fixed height with speed $100 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$. The magnitude of change in velocity after completing $1 / 2$ revolution is equal to 8 N (in $\mathrm{km} / \mathrm{hr}$ ). Find the value of N .
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Angular position $\theta$ of a particle moving on a curvilinear path varies according to $\theta=t^3-3 t^2+4 t-2$, where $\theta$ is in radians and time $t$ is in seconds. What is its average angular acceleration in the time interval $t=2 s$ to $t=4 s$ ?
questions.subject: Physics
